rationale: Both titles describe the same small feasibility series of ICE-guided LAA closure with the LAmbre device in patients with LAA thrombus. Y specifies 'Left Atrial Appendage Thrombus' in full, eliminating any ambiguity about which appendage structure harbors the thrombus. X shortens this to 'Appendage Thrombus,' which is incomplete and slightly imprecise as a standalone noun phrase in a title. X partially compensates by embedding the sample size ('12-Patient') in the subtitle, which adds numerical specificity for a small feasibility series; Y omits this detail. However, anatomical precision is more fundamental to title quality than sample-size disclosure, and readers can obtain n from the abstract. Y's superior clarity tips the overall judgment.
loser_weakness: X uses the truncated phrase 'Appendage Thrombus' instead of the full anatomical term 'Left Atrial Appendage Thrombus,' creating mild ambiguity in the title itself even though the context of LAA closure makes the referent inferable.
